kids.question: Who wrote this letter to the Thessalonians?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:1 — "Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians."

kids.question: What three things did Paul thank God the Thessalonians had?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:3 — "Your work of faith, and labour of love, and patience of hope."

kids.question: The Thessalonians turned from idols to serve whom?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:9 — "Ye turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God."

teens.question: How did the gospel come to the Thessalonians according to verse 5?
teens.option_a: Not in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Ghost
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:5 — "Our gospel came not unto you in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Ghost, and in much assurance."

teens.question: What did the Thessalonians become to believers in Macedonia and Achaia?
teens.option_a: Examples (ensamples) to all that believe
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:7 — "So that ye were ensamples to all that believe in Macedonia and Achaia."

teens.question: Despite receiving the word in much affliction, what did the Thessalonians have?
teens.option_a: Joy of the Holy Ghost
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:6 — "Having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Ghost."

teens.question: What sounded out from the Thessalonians in every place?
teens.option_a: The word of the Lord
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:8 — "For from you sounded out the word of the Lord not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place."

teens.question: Who are the Thessalonians waiting for from heaven?
teens.option_a: God's Son Jesus, who delivers us from the wrath to come
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:10 — "To wait for his Son from heaven... even Jesus, which delivered us from the wrath to come."

adults.question: Who are the co-senders of this epistle with Paul?
adults.option_a: Silvanus and Timotheus
adults.option_b: Barnabas and Luke
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:1 — "Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians."

adults.question: What does Paul say he knows about the Thessalonians' election in verse 4?
adults.option_a: Their election of God
adults.option_b: Their political elections
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:4 — "Knowing, brethren beloved, your election of God."

adults.question: Who did the Thessalonians become followers of according to verse 6?
adults.option_a: Paul and the Lord
adults.option_b: The Roman governors
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:6 — "And ye became followers of us, and of the Lord."

adults.question: What does Paul say the Thessalonians' faith had done geographically?
adults.option_a: Their faith to God-ward had spread abroad in every place
adults.option_b: Their faith was confined to Thessalonica
adults.option_d: Their faith was known only to Paul
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:8 — "Your faith to God-ward is spread abroad; so that we need not to speak any thing."

adults.question: What two actions describe the Thessalonians' conversion in verse 9?
adults.option_a: They turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God
adults.option_b: They left their homes and moved to Jerusalem
adults.option_c: They stopped eating and began fasting
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:9 — "Ye turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God."

adults.question: What eschatological hope is mentioned in verse 10?
adults.option_a: Waiting for God's Son from heaven whom he raised from the dead
adults.option_b: Waiting for the temple to be rebuilt
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:10 — "And to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead."

adults.question: What does Jesus deliver believers from according to verse 10?
adults.option_a: The wrath to come
adults.option_b: Physical death
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:10 — "Even Jesus, which delivered us from the wrath to come."

adults.question: What does Paul's mention of "much assurance" in verse 5 suggest about his ministry?
adults.option_a: The gospel was confirmed by the conviction of the Holy Spirit, not just human persuasion
adults.option_b: Paul was extremely confident in his own abilities
adults.option_d: Paul had official documents proving his authority
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:5 — "In power, and in the Holy Ghost, and in much assurance." This indicates the Spirit's confirming work in both preacher and hearers.

kids.question: How did Paul say he treated the Thessalonians?
kids.option_a: Gently, like a nursing mother caring for children
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:7 — "We were gentle among you, even as a nurse cherisheth her children."

kids.question: Did Paul work hard or was he lazy when he visited?
kids.option_a: He worked hard night and day
kids.option_b: He was lazy and did nothing
kids.option_c: He only worked one day a week
kids.option_d: He made others work for him
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:9 — "Labouring night and day, because we would not be chargeable unto any of you."

kids.question: What did Paul call the Thessalonians in verse 19?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:19 — "For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Are not even ye?"

teens.question: Where had Paul been shamefully treated before coming to Thessalonica?
teens.option_a: Philippi
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:2 — "Even after that we had suffered before, and were shamefully entreated, as ye know, at Philippi."

teens.question: What did Paul say he never used as a cloke (cover) for his motives?
teens.option_a: Flattering words or covetousness
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:5 — "For neither at any time used we flattering words, as ye know, nor a cloke of covetousness; God is witness."

teens.question: How does Paul describe a father's role with his children as a model for ministry?
teens.option_a: Exhorting, comforting, and charging them to walk worthy of God
teens.option_b: Punishing them severely for any mistake
teens.option_c: Leaving them to figure things out alone
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:11-12 — "As a father doth his children, Exhorting and comforting and charging... that ye would walk worthy of God."

teens.question: What did the Thessalonians receive Paul's message as?
teens.option_a: The word of God, which effectually worketh in believers
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:13 — "Ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which effectually worketh also in you that believe."

teens.question: Who does Paul say hindered him from coming to the Thessalonians?
teens.option_a: Satan
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:18 — "We would have come unto you, even I Paul, once and again; but Satan hindered us."

adults.question: What does Paul say about seeking glory from men in verse 6?
adults.option_a: They did not seek glory of men, neither of the Thessalonians nor of others
adults.option_b: They sought glory from the Roman authorities
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:6 — "Nor of men sought we glory, neither of you, nor yet of others."

adults.question: What apostolic right did Paul say he could have used but chose not to?
adults.option_a: Being burdensome (claiming financial support) as apostles of Christ
adults.option_b: Demanding the best seats in synagogues
adults.option_d: Insisting on special food and lodging
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:6-7 — "When we might have been burdensome, as the apostles of Christ. But we were gentle among you."

adults.question: What does Paul say the Jews did that filled up their sins in verse 16?
adults.option_a: Forbidding the apostles to speak to the Gentiles that they might be saved
adults.option_b: Rebuilding the temple without permission
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:16 — "Forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they might be saved, to fill up their sins alway."

adults.question: What does Paul say came upon the opposing Jews "to the uttermost"?
adults.option_a: Wrath
adults.option_b: Blessing
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:16 — "For the wrath is come upon them to the uttermost."

adults.question: What does Paul mean by being "taken from you" in verse 17?
adults.option_a: Being separated in presence but not in heart (orphaned from them physically)
adults.option_b: Dying and going to heaven
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:17 — "Being taken from you for a short time in presence, not in heart, endeavoured the more abundantly to see your face with great desire."

adults.question: Before whom does Paul say the Thessalonians are his crown of rejoicing?
adults.option_a: Before our Lord Jesus Christ at his coming
adults.option_b: Before the Roman emperor
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:19 — "Are not even ye in the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ at his coming?"

adults.question: What churches does Paul say the Thessalonians became followers of in verse 14?
adults.option_a: The churches of God in Judaea which are in Christ Jesus
adults.option_b: The churches in Rome
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:14 — "For ye, brethren, became followers of the churches of God which in Judaea are in Christ Jesus."

adults.question: What bold claim does Paul make about his preaching motive in verse 4?
adults.option_a: Not as pleasing men but God, which trieth our hearts
adults.option_b: To gain followers for his own ministry
adults.option_c: To become famous throughout the Roman Empire
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:4 — "But as we were allowed of God to be put in trust with the gospel, even so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, which trieth our hearts."

kids.question: Who did Paul send to check on the Thessalonians?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:2 — "And sent Timotheus, our brother, and minister of God."

kids.question: What good news did Timothy bring back about the Thessalonians?
kids.option_a: Good news of their faith and love
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:6 — "When Timotheus came from you unto us, and brought us good tidings of your faith and charity."

kids.question: How did Paul feel when he heard about their faith?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:7 — "We were comforted over you in all our affliction and distress by your faith."

teens.question: Why did Paul send Timothy to the Thessalonians?
teens.option_a: To establish and comfort them concerning their faith
teens.option_b: To collect money for the church in Jerusalem
teens.option_c: To start a new church nearby
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:2 — "To establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith."

teens.question: What did Paul fear about the Thessalonians?
teens.option_a: That the tempter had tempted them and his labour was in vain
teens.option_b: That they had become wealthy and proud
teens.option_c: That they had moved to another city
teens.option_d: That they had forgotten his name
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:5 — "Lest by some means the tempter have tempted you, and our labour be in vain."

teens.question: What did Paul say believers are appointed to in verse 3?
teens.option_a: Afflictions
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:3 — "That no man should be moved by these afflictions: for yourselves know that we are appointed thereunto."

teens.question: What does Paul say about their faith that he wants to perfect?
teens.option_a: That which is lacking in their faith
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:10 — "Night and day praying exceedingly that we might see your face, and might perfect that which is lacking in your faith."

teens.question: What does Paul pray would increase and abound among them?
teens.option_a: Love one toward another, and toward all men
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:12 — "The Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all men."

adults.question: Where was Paul left alone when he sent Timothy to Thessalonica?
adults.option_a: Athens
adults.option_b: Corinth
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:1 — "When we could no longer forbear, we thought it good to be left at Athens alone."

adults.question: What three titles does Paul give Timothy in verse 2?
adults.option_a: Brother, minister of God, and fellowlabourer in the gospel of Christ
adults.option_b: Apostle, prophet, and evangelist
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:2 — "Timotheus, our brother, and minister of God, and our fellowlabourer in the gospel of Christ."

adults.question: What does Paul say he told the Thessalonians beforehand?
adults.option_a: That they would suffer tribulation, which came to pass
adults.option_b: That they would become wealthy
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:4 — "When we were with you, we told you before that we should suffer tribulation; even as it came to pass."

adults.question: What does Paul say gives him life in verse 8?
adults.option_a: If ye stand fast in the Lord
adults.option_b: If they send him financial support
adults.option_c: If they build a new church
adults.option_d: If they convert the Roman governor
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:8 — "For now we live, if ye stand fast in the Lord."

adults.question: What is the goal of Paul's prayer in verse 13?
adults.option_a: To establish their hearts unblameable in holiness before God at the coming of the Lord Jesus
adults.option_b: To make them wealthy and prosperous
adults.option_d: To make them famous throughout the world
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:13 — "To the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before God, even our Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his saints."

adults.question: What does the reference to "the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his saints" in verse 13 affirm?
adults.option_a: The bodily return of Christ accompanied by all believers
adults.option_b: A spiritual metaphor for inner peace
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:13 — "At the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his saints." This affirms the literal Second Coming.

adults.question: What does Paul's phrase "we could no longer forbear" (v.1,5) reveal about his pastoral heart?
adults.option_a: His deep anxiety and love for the believers drove him to send Timothy despite his own need
adults.option_b: He was merely curious about their progress
adults.option_c: He was following an organizational protocol
adults.option_d: He was required by the church to check on them
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:1,5 — "When we could no longer forbear" shows Paul's intense concern for their spiritual welfare.

adults.question: What does Paul's thankfulness in verse 9 suggest about the relationship between a pastor and his flock?
adults.option_a: A pastor's deepest joy comes from the spiritual wellbeing of those he serves
adults.option_b: A pastor should seek financial reward
adults.option_c: A pastor should remain emotionally detached
adults.option_d: A pastor's joy depends on church size
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:9 — "For what thanks can we render to God again for you, for all the joy wherewith we joy for your sakes before our God."

kids.question: What does God want us to do according to Paul?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:3 — "For this is the will of God, even your sanctification."

kids.question: What will happen when Jesus comes back?
kids.option_a: The dead in Christ shall rise first
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:16 — "And the dead in Christ shall rise first."

kids.question: Where will believers meet the Lord?
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:17 — "Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air."

teens.question: What is the will of God according to verse 3?
teens.option_a: Your sanctification
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:3 — "For this is the will of God, even your sanctification."

teens.question: What does Paul say believers should do about those who have died (fallen asleep)?
teens.option_a: Not sorrow as others which have no hope
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:13 — "That ye sorrow not, even as others which have no hope."

teens.question: What three sounds accompany the Lord's descent from heaven?
teens.option_a: A shout, the voice of the archangel, and the trump of God
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:16 — "For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God."

teens.question: What should believers comfort each other with?
teens.option_a: These words about the Lord's return
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:18 — "Wherefore comfort one another with these words."

teens.question: How does Paul say believers should behave toward outsiders?
teens.option_a: Walk honestly and have lack of nothing
teens.option_c: Try to impress them with wealth
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:12 — "That ye may walk honestly toward them that are without, and that ye may have lack of nothing."

adults.question: What does Paul mean by "possess his vessel in sanctification and honour" in verse 4?
adults.option_a: Controlling one's own body in holiness and honor
adults.option_b: Owning a sacred artifact
adults.option_d: Having a boat for missionary journeys
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:4 — "That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour."

adults.question: What does Paul say God has called believers to in verse 7?
adults.option_a: Holiness, not uncleanness
adults.option_b: Wealth and prosperity
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:7 — "For God hath not called us unto uncleanness, but unto holiness."

adults.question: What does Paul say about rejecting these instructions in verse 8?
adults.option_a: He that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God who hath given us his Holy Spirit
adults.option_b: It is just Paul's personal opinion
adults.option_d: It only applies to church leaders
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:8 — "He therefore that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his holy Spirit."

adults.question: What does Paul say about brotherly love being taught?
adults.option_a: They are taught of God to love one another
adults.option_b: They learned it from Greek philosophy
adults.option_c: They were taught by Roman educators
adults.option_d: They figured it out on their own
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:9 — "As touching brotherly love ye need not that I write unto you: for ye yourselves are taught of God to love one another."

adults.question: What three practical instructions does Paul give in verse 11?
adults.option_a: Study to be quiet, do your own business, work with your own hands
adults.option_b: Preach loudly, meddle in others' affairs, avoid manual labor
adults.option_c: Travel widely, seek adventure, avoid routine
adults.option_d: Debate fiercely, lead politically, accumulate wealth
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:11 — "That ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you."

adults.question: What is the theological significance of the phrase "the dead in Christ shall rise first" (v.16)?
adults.option_a: Deceased believers have priority in the resurrection order, followed by living believers being caught up
adults.option_b: Only dead believers will be resurrected
adults.option_c: Living believers are not included in the rapture
adults.option_d: The dead in Christ are already in heaven
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:16 — "And the dead in Christ shall rise first." This establishes the order: resurrection of dead believers, then transformation of living believers.

adults.question: What does the phrase "caught up together" (harpazo in Greek) in verse 17 mean?
adults.option_a: Snatched away suddenly and forcefully — the basis for the doctrine of the rapture
adults.option_b: A gradual ascension over many days
adults.option_d: A political movement to overthrow Rome
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:17 — "Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds." The Greek harpazo means to seize or snatch away suddenly.

adults.question: What is the ultimate promise in verse 17 about believers' relationship with the Lord?
adults.option_a: So shall we ever be with the Lord — eternal, unbroken fellowship
adults.option_b: We will serve the Lord for 1,000 years
adults.option_c: We will be sent on missions throughout the universe
adults.option_d: We will sleep until the final judgment
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:17 — "And so shall we ever be with the Lord." The ultimate hope is permanent, unending communion with Christ.

kids.question: How will the day of the Lord come?
kids.option_a: Like a thief in the night
kids.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:2 — "The day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night."

kids.question: What does Paul say to do always?
What does Paul say about praying?
kids.option_d: Pray only when you are sad

teens.question: What will people be saying when sudden destruction comes?
teens.option_a: Peace and safety
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:3 — "For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them."

teens.question: What are believers called children of in verse 5?
teens.option_a: Children of light and children of the day
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:5 — "Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day."

teens.question: What spiritual armor does Paul mention in verse 8?
teens.option_a: The breastplate of faith and love, and the helmet of the hope of salvation
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:8 — "Putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for an helmet, the hope of salvation."

teens.question: What three short commands does Paul give in verses 16-18?
teens.option_a: Rejoice evermore, pray without ceasing, in every thing give thanks
teens.option_b: Fight bravely, run fast, eat well
teens.option_c: Study hard, work harder, sleep little
teens.option_d: Speak loudly, act boldly, think wisely
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18 — "Rejoice evermore. Pray without ceasing. In every thing give thanks."

teens.question: What does Paul say about the Spirit in verse 19?
teens.option_a: Quench not the Spirit
teens.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:19 — "Quench not the Spirit."

adults.question: What analogy does Paul use for sudden destruction in verse 3?
adults.option_a: Travail upon a woman with child — they shall not escape
adults.option_b: A flood sweeping away a village
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:3 — "Then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape."

adults.question: What does Paul say God has NOT appointed believers to in verse 9?
adults.option_a: Wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ
adults.option_b: Suffering of any kind
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:9 — "For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ."

adults.question: Whether we wake or sleep, what does Paul say in verse 10?
adults.option_a: We should live together with him
adults.option_b: We should fear judgment
adults.option_d: We should withdraw from the world
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:10 — "Who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him."

adults.question: What does "in every thing give thanks" in verse 18 mean in context?
adults.option_a: This is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you
adults.option_b: It is Paul's personal suggestion
adults.option_c: It only applies in good circumstances
adults.option_d: It is optional for mature believers
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:18 — "In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you."

adults.question: What does Paul say to do with prophecies in verse 20?
adults.option_a: Despise not prophesyings
adults.option_b: Accept all prophecies without question
adults.option_c: Write all prophecies in a book
adults.option_d: Only listen to prophecy from apostles
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:20 — "Despise not prophesyings."

adults.question: What does Paul say to prove (test) in verse 21?
adults.option_a: All things; hold fast that which is good
adults.option_b: Only teachings from apostles
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:21 — "Prove all things; hold fast that which is good."

adults.question: What does Paul pray to be preserved blameless in verse 23?
adults.option_a: Your whole spirit and soul and body unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ
adults.option_b: Only the spirit
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:23 — "I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ."

adults.question: What is the theological significance of the trichotomy "spirit and soul and body" in verse 23?
adults.option_a: It affirms the three-part nature of human beings: spirit (God-consciousness), soul (self-consciousness), and body (world-consciousness)
adults.option_b: It suggests humans have three separate bodies
adults.option_c: It is a poetic expression with no doctrinal significance
adults.option_d: It refers to three stages of Christian growth
adults.explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:23 — "Your whole spirit and soul and body." This verse is a key text in the trichotomist view of human nature, distinguishing spirit, soul, and body as distinct aspects of the person.
